DestinationMetadata.description:
Specifies if/how communication will be delivered to the customer.

You can configure the issue to contact the requester via email, Slack, in-app chat, SMS, WhatsApp, or not at all.

If a destination other than `internal` is specified, a message with the issue's BodyHTML will be delivered to the requester.

If you don't wish to immediately contact the requester, you can specify the `internal` destination.

chat_widget_app_id.description: The ID of the chat widget app to use for in-app chat.

destination.description:
The destination type of the issue. Can be "email", "slack", "in_app_chat", "sms", "whatsapp", or "internal". Defaults to "internal".

`slack`: Deliver to the issue's account's Slack channel.

`email`: Deliver messages to the issue requester via email.

`in_app_chat`: Deliver messages to the issue requester via in-app chat.

`internal`: Keep the issue internal and don't contact the requester at all.

`sms`: Deliver messages to the issue requester via SMS.

`whatsapp`: Deliver messages to the issue requester via WhatsApp.
destination.x-go-enum-desc:
slack APIIssueDestinationSlack  Deliver to the issue's account's Slack channel.
email APIIssueDestinationEmail  Deliver messages to the issue requester via email.
in_app_chat APIIssueDestinationChatWidget  Deliver messages to the issue requester via in-app chat.
internal APIIssueDestinationInternal  Keep the issue internal and don't contact the requester at all.
sms APIIssueDestinationSMS  Deliver messages to the issue requester via SMS.
whatsapp APIIssueDestinationWhatsApp  Deliver messages to the issue requester via WhatsApp.

field.description:
The field for this filter.  For allowed fields, see the documentation for

the specific endpoint you are using.

For non-compound filters (any operators other than "and" or "or"),

Field must be set, along with either Value or Values, depending on the operator.

operator.description:
The operator for this filter.

`equals`: Matches objects that are exactly equal to the value in the field.

`not_equals`: Matches objects that are not exactly equal to the value in the field.

`contains`: For a multi-valued field, such as tags, this operator will match  objects that contain any of the values in the field.

`does_not_contain`: For a multi-valued field, such as tags, this operator will match  objects that do not contain any of the values in the field.

`in`: Matches objects if the field is one of several possible values,  as specified in the values array.

`not_in`: Matches objects if the field is none of several possible values,  as specified in the values array.

`and`: Matches objects only if all subfilters match.

`or`: Matches objects if any subfilter matches.

`time_is_after`: Matches objects if the field is after the given time.

`time_is_before`: Matches objects if the field is before the given time.

`time_range`: Matches objects if the field is between the given times.

`string_contains`: Matches objects if the field contains the given string.

`string_does_not_contain`: Matches objects if the field does not contain the given string.

`is_set`: Matches objects if the field is set.

`is_unset`: Matches objects if the field is unset.

`greater_than`: Matches objects if the numeric field is greater than the given value.

`less_than`: Matches objects if the numeric field is less than the given value.

`greater_than_or_equals`: Matches objects if the numeric field is greater than or equal to the given value.

`less_than_or_equals`: Matches objects if the numeric field is less than or equal to the given value.
operator.x-go-enum-desc:

not_equals FilterOperatorNotEquals  Matches objects that are not exactly equal to the value in the field.
contains FilterOperatorContains  For a multi-valued field, such as tags, this operator will match  objects that contain any of the values in the field.
does_not_contain FilterOperatorDoesNotContain  For a multi-valued field, such as tags, this operator will match  objects that do not contain any of the values in the field.
in FilterOperatorIn  Matches objects if the field is one of several possible values,  as specified in the values array.
not_in FilterOperatorNotIn  Matches objects if the field is none of several possible values,  as specified in the values array.
and FilterOperatorAnd  Matches objects only if all subfilters match.
or FilterOperatorOr  Matches objects if any subfilter matches.
time_is_after FilterOperatorTimeIsAfter  Matches objects if the field is after the given time.
time_is_before FilterOperatorTimeIsBefore  Matches objects if the field is before the given time.
time_range FilterOperatorTimeRange  Matches objects if the field is between the given times.
string_contains FilterOperatorStringContains  Matches objects if the field contains the given string.
string_does_not_contain FilterOperatorStringDoesNotContain  Matches objects if the field does not contain the given string.
is_set FilterOperatorIsSet  FilterOperatorIsSet matches objects if the field is set.

external_ids.description:

Used for duplicate detection and updates.

file_names.description:
Custom names for the uploaded files. Must match the number of files if provided.

If not provided, original filenames will be used.

files.description:
The files to upload (minimum 1, maximum 10).

Supported types: PDF, plain text, markdown, CSV, JSON, and images (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP).

Maximum size: 50MB per file, 100MB total.

training_data_id.description:
The ID of an existing training data container to upload files to.

Required if not creating new training data.

training_data_name.description:
The name of a new training data container.

Required when creating new training data (if training_data_id is not provided).

visibility.description:
Visibility of the training data. Defaults to "everyone".
